children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The purpose of this section is to improve compliance with, and the integrity of, the Federal crop insurance program.

children.heading: Role of insurance providers
children.label: 2 Role of insurance providers
children.text: The Corporation shall work actively with approved insurance providers to address program compliance and integrity issues as such issues develop.

children.heading: Notification of errors, omissions, and failures
children.label: 1 Notification of errors, omissions, and failures
children.text: The Corporation shall notify in writing an approved insurance provider of any error, omission, or failure to follow Corporation regulations or procedures for which the approved insurance provider may be responsible and which may result in a debt owed the Corporation.

children.heading: Time for notification
children.label: 2 Time for notification
children.text: Notice under paragraph (1) shall be given within 3 years after the end of the insurance period during which the error, omission, or failure is alleged to have occurred, except that this time limitation shall not apply with respect to an error, omission, or procedural violation that is willful or intentional.

children.heading: Effect of failure to timely notify
children.label: 3 Effect of failure to timely notify
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the failure to timely provide the notice required under this subsection shall relieve the approved insurance provider from the debt owed the Corporation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall develop and implement a coordinated plan for the Corporation and the Farm Service Agency to reconcile all relevant information received by the Corporation or the Farm Service Agency from a producer who obtains crop insurance coverage under this subchapter.

children.heading: Frequency
children.label: 2 Frequency
children.text: Beginning with the 2001 crop year, the Secretary shall require that the Corporation and the Farm Service Agency reconcile such producer-derived information on at least an annual basis in order to identify and address any discrepancies.

children.label: i
children.text: within a reasonable amount of time following the applicable sales closing date, to correct errors in information that is provided by a producer for the purpose of obtaining coverage under any policy or plan of insurance made available under this subchapter to ensure that the eligibility information is correct and consistent with information reported by the producer for other programs administered by the Secretary;

children.label: I
children.text: the acreage reporting date, to reconcile errors in the information reported by the producer with correct information determined from any other program administered by the Secretary; or

children.label: II
children.text: the date of any subsequent correction of data by the Farm Service Agency made as a result of the verification of information, to make conforming corrections; and

children.label: iii
children.text: at any time, to correct electronic transmission errors that were made by an agent or approved insurance provider, or such errors made by the Farm Service Agency or any other agency of the Department of Agriculture in transmitting the information provided by the producer for purposes of other programs of the Department to the extent an agent or approved insurance provider relied upon the erroneous information for crop insurance purposes.

children.label: i
children.text: to avoid ineligibility requirements for insurance or obtain a disproportionate benefit under the crop insurance program or any related program administered by the Secretary;

children.label: ii
children.text: to obtain, enhance, or increase an insurance guarantee or indemnity if a cause of loss exists or has occurred before any correction has been made, or avoid premium owed if no loss is likely to occur; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to avoid an obligation or requirement under any Federal or State law.

children.heading: Exception to late filing sanctions
children.label: C Exception to late filing sanctions
children.text: Any corrections made within a reasonable amount of time, in accordance with established procedures, pursuant to this paragraph shall not be subject to any late filing sanctions authorized in the reinsurance agreement with the Corporation.

children.heading: Late payment of debt
children.label: D Late payment of debt
children.text: In the case of a producer that has inadvertently failed to pay a debt due as specified by regulations of the Corporation and has been determined to be ineligible for crop insurance pursuant to the terms of the policy as a result of that failure, the Corporation may determine to allow the producer to pay the debt and purchase the crop insurance after the sales closing date, in accordance with procedures and limitations established by the Corporation.

children.heading: Identification and elimination of fraud, waste, and abuse
children.label: d Identification and elimination of fraud, waste, and abuse
A
children.text: at the request of the Corporation or, subject to paragraph (2), on its own initiative if the Farm Service Agency has reason to suspect the existence of program fraud, waste, or abuse, conducting fact finding relative to allegations of program fraud, waste, or abuse;

children.label: B
children.text: reporting to the Corporation, in writing in a timely manner, the results of any fact finding conducted pursuant to subparagraph (A), any allegation of fraud, waste, or abuse, and any identified program vulnerabilities;

children.label: C
children.text: assisting the Corporation and approved insurance providers in auditing a statistically appropriate number of claims made under any policy or plan of insurance under this subchapter; and

children.label: i
children.text: detect yield disparities or other data anomalies that indicate potential fraud; and

children.label: ii
children.text: target the relevant counties, crops, regions, companies, or agents associated with that potential fraud for audits and other enforcement actions.

children.heading: FSA inquiry
children.label: 2 FSA inquiry
children.text: If, within five calendar days after receiving a report submitted under paragraph (1)(B), the Corporation does not provide a written response that describes the intended actions of the Corporation, the Farm Service Agency may conduct its own inquiry into the alleged program fraud, waste, or abuse on approval from the State director of the Farm Service Agency of the State in which the alleged fraud, waste, or abuse occurred. If as a result of the inquiry, the Farm Service Agency concludes further investigation is warranted, but the Corporation declines to proceed with the investigation, the Farm Service Agency may refer the matter to the Inspector General of the Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: Use of field infrastructure
children.label: 3 Use of field infrastructure
children.text: The plan required by paragraph (1) shall provide for the use of the field infrastructure of the Farm Service Agency. The Secretary shall ensure that relevant Farm Service Agency personnel are appropriately trained for any responsibilities assigned to the personnel under the plan. At a minimum, the personnel shall receive the same level of training and pass the same basic competency tests as required of loss adjusters of approved insurance providers.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The activities of the Farm Service Agency under this subsection do not affect the responsibility of approved insurance providers to conduct any audits of claims or other program reviews required by the Corporation.

children.heading: Notification of providers
children.label: B Notification of providers
children.text: The Corporation shall notify the appropriate approved insurance provider of a report from the Farm Service Agency regarding alleged program fraud, waste, or abuse, unless the provider is suspected to be included in, or a party to, the alleged fraud, waste, or abuse.

children.heading: Response
children.label: C Response
children.text: An approved insurance provider that receives a notice under subparagraph (B) shall submit a report to the Corporation, within an appropriate time period determined by the Secretary, describing the actions taken by the provider to investigate the allegations of program fraud, waste, or abuse contained in the notice.

children.heading: Prompt response
children.label: A Prompt response
children.text: If an approved insurance provider reports to the Corporation that the approved insurance provider suspects intentional misrepresentation, fraud, waste, or abuse, the Corporation shall make a determination and provide, within 90 calendar days after receiving the report, a written response that describes the intended actions of the Corporation.

children.heading: Cooperative effort
children.label: B Cooperative effort
children.text: The approved insurance provider and the Corporation shall take coordinated action in any case where misrepresentation, fraud, waste, or abuse is alleged.

children.heading: Failure to timely respond
children.label: C Failure to timely respond
children.text: If the Corporation fails to respond as required by subparagraph (A), an approved insurance provider may request the Farm Service Agency to assist the provider in an inquiry into the alleged program fraud, waste, or abuse.

children.heading: Consultation with State FSA committees
children.label: e Consultation with State FSA committees
children.text: The Secretary shall establish procedures under which the Corporation shall consult with the State committee of the Farm Service Agency for a State with respect to policies, plans of insurance, and material related to such policies or plans of insurance (including applicable sales closing dates, assigned yields, and transitional yields) offered in that State under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: Any agent engaged in the sale of coverage offered under this subchapter where the loss claims associated with such sales by the agent are equal to or greater than 150 percent (or an appropriate percentage specified by the Corporation) of the mean for all loss claims associated with such sales by all other agents operating in the same area, as determined by the Corporation.

children.label: B
children.text: Any person performing loss adjustment services relative to coverage offered under this subchapter where such loss adjustments performed by the person result in accepted or denied claims equal to or greater than 150 percent (or an appropriate percentage specified by the Corporation) of the mean for accepted or denied claims (as applicable) for all other persons performing loss adjustment services in the same area, as determined by the Corporation.

children.heading: Review required
children.label: A Review required
children.text: The Corporation shall conduct a review of any agent identified under paragraph (1)(A), and any person identified under paragraph (1)(B), to determine whether the higher loss claims associated with the agent or the higher number of accepted or denied claims (as applicable) associated with the person are the result of fraud, waste, or abuse.

children.heading: Remedial action
children.label: B Remedial action
children.text: The Corporation shall take appropriate remedial action with respect to any occurrence of fraud, waste, or abuse identified in a review conducted under this paragraph.

children.heading: Oversight of agents and loss adjusters
children.label: 3 Oversight of agents and loss adjusters
children.text: The Corporation shall develop procedures to require an annual review by an approved insurance provider of the performance of each agent and loss adjuster used by the approved insurance provider. The Corporation shall oversee the conduct of annual reviews and may consult with an approved insurance provider regarding any remedial action that is determined to be necessary as a result of the annual review of an agent or loss adjuster.

children.heading: Submission of information to Corporation to support compliance efforts
children.label: g Submission of information to Corporation to support compliance efforts
A
children.text: The name and identification number of the insured.

children.label: B
children.text: The agricultural commodity to be insured.

children.label: C
children.text: The elected coverage level, including the price election, of the insured.

children.label: D
children.text: The actual production history to be used to establish insurable yields.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The information required to be submitted under subparagraphs (A) through (C) of paragraph (1) with respect to a policy or plan of insurance shall be submitted so as to ensure receipt by the Corporation not later than the Saturday of the week containing the calendar day that is 30 days after the applicable sales closing date for the crop to be insured.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The information required to be submitted under paragraph (1)(D) with respect to an applicable policy or plan of insurance for a covered commodity (as defined in ) shall be submitted so as to ensure receipt by the Corporation not later than the Saturday of the week containing the calendar day that is 30 days after the applicable production reporting date for the crop to be insured.section 9011 of this title

children.heading: Correction of errors
children.label: ii Correction of errors
children.text: Nothing in clause (i) limits the ability of an approved insurance provider to correct any error in the information submitted under paragraph (1)(D) after receipt of the information by the Corporation in accordance with clause (i).

children.heading: Sanctions for program noncompliance and fraud
children.label: h Sanctions for program noncompliance and fraud

children.heading: False information
children.label: 1 False information
children.text: A producer, agent, loss adjuster, approved insurance provider, or other person that willfully and intentionally provides any false or inaccurate information to the Corporation or to an approved insurance provider with respect to a policy or plan of insurance under this subchapter may, after notice and an opportunity for a hearing on the record, be subject to one or more of the sanctions described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: Compliance
children.label: 2 Compliance
children.text: A person may, after notice and an opportunity for a hearing on the record, be subject to one or more of the sanctions described in paragraph (3) if the person is a producer, agent, loss adjuster, approved insurance provider, or other person that willfully and intentionally fails to comply with a requirement of the Corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of the pecuniary gain obtained as a result of the false or inaccurate information provided or the noncompliance with a requirement of this subchapter; or

children.label: i
children.text: This subchapter.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Agricultural Market Transition Act ( et seq.), including the noninsured crop disaster assistance program under section 196 of that Act ().7 U.S.C. 72017 U.S.C. 7333

children.label: iii
children.text: The Agricultural Act of 1949 ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 1421

children.label: iv
children.text: The Commodity Credit Corporation Charter Act ( et seq.).15 U.S.C. 714

children.label: v
children.text: The Agricultural Adjustment Act of 1938 ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 1281

children.label: vi
children.text: Title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 3801

children.label: vii
children.text: The Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 1921

children.label: viii
children.text: Any law that provides assistance to a producer of an agricultural commodity affected by a crop loss or a decline in the prices of agricultural commodities.

children.heading: Disqualification of other persons
children.label: C Disqualification of other persons
children.text: In the case of a violation committed by an agent, loss adjuster, approved insurance provider, or other person (other than a producer), the violator may be disqualified for a period of up to 5 years from participating in any program, or receiving any benefit, under this subchapter.

children.label: A
children.text: whether to impose a sanction under this subsection; and

children.label: B
children.text: the type and amount of the sanction to be imposed.

children.label: A
children.text: providing false or inaccurate information to the Corporation or to an approved insurance provider; or

children.label: B
children.text: failing to comply with a requirement of the Corporation.

children.heading: Insurance fund
children.label: 6 Insurance fund
children.text: Any funds collected under this subsection shall be deposited into the insurance fund established under .section 1516(c) of this title

children.heading: Annual report on program compliance and integrity efforts
children.label: i Annual report on program compliance and integrity efforts

children.heading: Report required
children.label: 1 Report required
children.text: The Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate an annual report describing the operation of this section during the preceding year and efforts undertaken by the Secretary and the Corporation to carry out this section.

children.heading: Information regarding fraud, waste, and abuse
children.label: 2 Information regarding fraud, waste, and abuse
children.text: The report shall identify specific occurrences of waste, fraud, or abuse and contain an outline of actions that have been or are being taken to eliminate the identified waste, fraud, or abuse.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall maintain and upgrade the information management systems of the Corporation used in the administration and enforcement of this subchapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In maintaining and upgrading the systems, the Secretary shall ensure that new hardware and software are compatible with the hardware and software used by other agencies of the Department to maximize data sharing and promote the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Acreage report streamlining initiative project
children.label: ii Acreage report streamlining initiative project
children.text: As soon as practicable, the Secretary shall develop and implement an acreage report streamlining initiative project to allow producers to report acreage and other information directly to the Department.

children.heading: Use of available information technologies
children.label: 2 Use of available information technologies
children.text: The Secretary shall use the information technologies known as data mining and data warehousing and other available information technologies to administer and enforce this subchapter.

children.heading: Use of private sector
children.label: 3 Use of private sector
children.text: The Secretary may enter into contracts to use private sector expertise and technological resources in implementing this subsection, which shall be subject to competition on a periodic basis, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Continuing education for loss adjusters and agents
children.label: k Continuing education for loss adjusters and agents

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Corporation shall establish requirements for continuing education for loss adjusters and agents of approved insurance providers.

children.label: A
children.text: the policies and plans of insurance available under this subchapter, including the regulations promulgated to carry out this subchapter;

children.label: B
children.text: efforts to promote program integrity through the elimination of waste, fraud, and abuse; and

children.label: C
children.text: other aspects of adjusting, delivering, and servicing policies and plans of insurance by adjustors and agents, as determined by the Secretary, including conservation activities and agronomic practices (including organic and sustainable practices) that are common and appropriate to the area in which the insured crop being inspected is produced.

children.label: I
children.text: for fiscal year 2014, $14,000,000; and

children.label: II
children.text: for each of fiscal years 2015 through 2018, $9,000,000; or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the Acreage Crop Reporting Streamlining Initiative (ACRSI) project is substantially completed by , not more than $14,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2015 through 2018.September 30, 2015

children.heading: Notification
children.label: B Notification
children.text: The Secretary shall notify the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate of the substantial completion of the Acreage Crop Reporting Streamlining Initiative (ACRSI) project not later than .July 1, 2015

children.label: A
children.text: $4,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2025; and

children.label: B
children.text: $6,000,000 for fiscal year 2026 and each subsequent fiscal year.
